task_completion.description: Open the Tor Browser cookie exceptions dialog
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Exceptions - Cookies and Site Data' dialog in Tor Browser settings is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Add 'Allow' exceptions for the specified domains
task_completion.visual_evidence: The domains 'check.torproject.org', 'duckduckgo.com', and 'en.wikipedia.org' (or their https:// equivalents) are visible in the Exceptions list with the status 'Allow'.

task_completion.description: Add 'Block' exceptions for the specified domains
task_completion.visual_evidence: The domains 'www.facebook.com' and 'www.google.com' (or their https:// equivalents) are visible in the Exceptions list with the status 'Block'.

task_completion.description: Verify the policy by visiting the test sites
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Tor Browser is seen navigating to 'https://check.torproject.org' and 'https://duckduckgo.com', either in separate tabs or sequentially in the address bar.

task_completion.description: Create the cookie policy report file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano, or vim) or terminal shows the creation or editing of a file named 'cookie_policy_report.txt' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

task_completion.description: Write the correct content into the report file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of the report file include the exact header 'Cookie Policy Configuration Report' and correctly list all five domains with their respective 'Allow' or 'Block' statuses.

integrity.description: The agent used the Tor Browser GUI to configure exceptions
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Tor Browser settings menu and Exceptions dialog, rather than terminal commands modifying sqlite databases or profile files directly.

integrity.description: The agent used the browser to visit the test sites
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Tor Browser rendering the test websites, rather than terminal commands like 'curl' or 'wget' being used to fetch the pages.

integrity.description: The agent created the report file genuinely
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen typing or pasting the content into a text editor or using standard terminal commands (e.g., echo, cat) to create the file, rather than executing a pre-existing script.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Allows: check.torproject.org, duckduckgo.com, en.wikipedia.org. Expected Blocks: www.facebook.com, www.google.com. Report Path: /home/ga/Documents/cookie_policy_report.txt
